lines.en: From Houston, Mission Control sent a goodbye message.
lines.zh: 休斯顿的任务控制中心发来告别信息。

lines.en: Integrity, Houston.
lines.zh: 诚信号，休斯顿呼叫。

lines.en: You are
lines.zh: 你现在

lines.en: six minutes from your 40-minute lunar flyby
lines.zh: 距离 40 分钟的月球飞掠还有 6 分钟

lines.en: LOS.
lines.zh: 信号中断。

lines.en: From all of us, it's a privilege
lines.zh: 我们所有人都觉得很荣幸

lines.en: to witness you carrying the fire past our farthest reach.
lines.zh: 能见证你把火种带到我们最远的地方。

lines.en: Thank you.
lines.zh: 谢谢。

lines.en: Godspeed.
lines.zh: 一路顺风。

lines.en: This loss of signal was a planned event.
lines.zh: 这次信号中断是计划好的。

lines.en: There's just no way to get a communications signal through the moon.
lines.zh: 根本没法把通讯信号穿过月球。

lines.en: Pilot Victor Glover responded.
lines.zh: 飞行员维克多·格洛弗回应了。

lines.en: Thank you for that, Jenny.
lines.zh: 谢谢你，Jenny。

lines.en: And thank you to all of you for
lines.zh: 也谢谢大家

lines.en: allowing us the immense privilege to be on this journey together.
lines.zh: 让我们有幸一起踏上这段旅程。

lines.en: It's quite amazing.
lines.zh: 真是太棒了。

lines.en: And as we
lines.zh: 而我们

lines.en: go on this journey, thinking about the NASA mission to
lines.zh: 在这段旅程中，也想到NASA的任务是

lines.en: explore the unknown in air and space, to innovate
lines.zh: 探索空中和太空的未知，进行创新

lines.en: for the benefit of humanity, and to inspire the world through discovery.
lines.zh: 造福人类，并通过发现去激励全世界。

lines.en: And as you've gone along on this journey with us, hopefully we're doing just
lines.zh: 一路跟我们走到现在，希望我们做得还不错

lines.en: those things.
lines.zh: 就是这些事

lines.en: And as we get close to the nearest point to the moon
lines.zh: 当我们接近离月亮最近的地方

lines.en: and farthest point from Earth, as we continue to unlock
lines.zh: 和离地球最远的地方，继续探索

lines.en: the mysteries of the cosmos, I would like
lines.zh: 宇宙的奥秘时，我想

lines.en: to remind you of one of the most important mysteries there on
lines.zh: 提醒你地球上一个最重要的谜

lines.en: Earth, and that's love.
lines.zh: 那就是爱

lines.en: Christ said
lines.zh: 基督说过

lines.en: in response to what was the greatest command, that it was to love God with all that you
lines.zh: 有人问最大的诫命是什么，他回答是要全心全意爱上帝

lines.en: are.
lines.zh: 用你的一切去爱

lines.en: And he also,
lines.zh: 他还说

lines.en: being a great teacher, said the second is equal to it, and that is to love your
lines.zh: 作为一位伟大的老师，他说第二条同样重要，就是要爱你的

lines.en: neighbor as yourself.
lines.zh: 邻居像爱自己一样

lines.en: And so
lines.zh: 所以

lines.en: as we prepare to go out of radio communication, we're still
lines.zh: 当我们准备结束无线电通讯时，我们依然

lines.en: able to feel your love from Earth
lines.zh: 能感受到来自地球的爱

lines.en: and to all of you down there on Earth
lines.zh: 也感受到地球上所有人的爱

lines.en: and around Earth, we love you from the moon.
lines.zh: 我们从月球爱你们

questions.question: What's the main idea of the speaker's message?

lines.zh: 就这样，宇航员们彻底

lines.en: alone.
lines.zh: 孤身一人。

lines.zh: NASA的宇航员里德·怀斯曼，

lines.en: Victor Glover, and Christina Cook, and Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen.
lines.zh: 维克多·格洛弗、克里斯蒂娜·库克，还有加拿大宇航员杰里米·汉森。

lines.zh: 他们漂在飞船里，离地球有二十五万多英里。

lines.en: seeing parts of the moon with unaided eyes for the first time
lines.zh: 第一次用肉眼看到月球的某些地方，

lines.en: in human history.

lines.zh: 四十分钟后，宇航员们

lines.en: regained contact.
lines.zh: 重新联系上了。

lines.zh: 任务专家克里斯蒂娜·库克通过无线电过来了。

lines.en: Houston, Integrity.
lines.zh: 休斯顿，这里是诚信号。

lines.en: Comm check.

lines.en: you too.

lines.en: When we burned this burn towards the moon,
lines.zh: 当我们把这团火烧向月亮时，

clips.info_takeaway: 飞船重返地球时速度可达4万公里/小时，摩擦产生近5000华氏度高温，热盾保护宇航员安全。

lines.en: Their spacecraft, which is called Orion, is traveling about 25,000
lines.zh: 他们的飞船叫猎户座，正以大约每小时2.5万英里飞行

lines.en: miles an hour when it begins to feel the effects of Earth's atmosphere.
lines.zh: 开始感受到地球大气的影响

lines.zh: 那可是每小时超过4万公里

lines.en: or fast enough to fly from New York to Tokyo in less than 20 minutes.

lines.zh: 大气摩擦让温度升到

lines.en: communication, and the astronauts see flashes of plasma dance

lines.en: during a test flight of Orion with nobody on board.

clips.title: 八岁男孩的登月记忆
clips.info_takeaway: 1969年登月时，墨西哥男孩被父亲叫醒观看电视直播，留下深刻记忆。

source.episode: Artemis: Why Are We Really Going Back to the Moon?

lines.en: He was eight years old, living in Chihuahua, Mexico,
lines.zh: 他那年八岁，住在墨西哥的奇瓦瓦。

lines.en: and his dad woke him up to see it.
lines.zh: 他爸把他叫醒来看这个。

lines.en: And I just remember very clearly my dad shaking me,
lines.zh: 我清楚记得我爸摇醒我。

lines.en: OK, come on, you got to watch this.
lines.zh: 好，快起来，你得看看这个。

lines.en: What do you remember from seeing on the screen?
lines.zh: 你记得屏幕上看到什么吗？

lines.en: It was a small black and white TV and it was this grainy picture
lines.zh: 是一台小小的黑白电视，画面很模糊。

lines.en: and I start watching and I see the humans stepping out.
lines.zh: 我开始看，看到人类走出来。

lines.en: That's one small step for man.
lines.zh: 这是人类的一小步。

lines.en: One giant leap for mankind.
lines.zh: 却是人类的一大步。

lines.en: My dad keeps saying, this is a momentous occasion.
lines.zh: 我爸一直说，这是个重要时刻。

lines.en: Oh, we were talking about it the day after,
lines.zh: 哦，我们第二天还在聊这事儿

lines.en: everybody was talking about it.
lines.zh: 大家都在聊

lines.en: And my uncle, my aunts were like, did you watch this?
lines.zh: 我叔叔、我姑姑都问我：你看了吗？

lines.en: It was incredible.

lines.en: I mean, we made it to the moon.

lines.en: How far we have gone.

clips.title: 为什么理性也挡不住债务

source.episode: The Debt Trap

lines.en: It happens all the time.

lines.en: The lovely couple with a nice house, beautiful family and gainful careers
lines.zh: 那对有房、有家、有好工作的恩爱夫妻

lines.en: unexpectedly find themselves drowning in tens of thousands of dollars of credit card debt.
lines.zh: 突然发现自己欠了几万美元的信用卡债。

lines.zh: 到2024年底，债务增加了930亿美元

lines.en: and half of that increase came from new credit card debt.
lines.zh: 其中一半是新的信用卡债。

lines.en: Now New York Federal Reserve.
lines.zh: 现在是纽约联储的数据。

lines.en: Or maybe it's the fresh college graduate who rather than looking forward to a bright future
lines.zh: 或者是刚毕业的大学生，本该期待光明的未来

lines.en: and a sense of freedom finds himself trying to pay back a series of compounding loans
lines.zh: 却要拼命还一堆利滚利的贷款

lines.en: only to watch the balance grow year after year after year.
lines.zh: 结果每年都看着欠款越涨越多。

lines.en: One out of every four Americans with student loans
lines.zh: 每四个有学生贷款的美国人里就有一个

lines.en: is delinquent.
lines.zh: 拖欠了。

lines.en: That's nearly triple the pre-pandemic delinquency rates.
lines.zh: 这几乎是疫情前拖欠率的三倍。

lines.en: And then there's the retiree who thought she had enough saved
lines.zh: 还有一个退休的人，以为自己存够了钱

lines.en: until one medical emergency wiped out her nest egg.
lines.zh: 结果一次医疗急事就把老本全花光了。

lines.en: And so you just start chipping away at your savings,
lines.zh: 于是你就开始一点点动用存款，

lines.en: chipping away at everything you have and ultimately chipping away at retirement.
lines.zh: 一点点耗掉所有的积蓄，最后连退休金都没了。

lines.en: It's easy to believe this would never happen to me.
lines.zh: 很容易觉得这种事不会发生在我身上。

lines.en: I would be smarter, savvier, better at saving.
lines.zh: 我会更聪明、更精明、更会存钱。

lines.en: And yet hundreds of millions of people around the world find themselves enmeshed in debt.
lines.zh: 可全世界还是有几亿人陷入债务。

lines.en: Some of this has to do with reckless spending of course.
lines.zh: 当然，这里面有些是因为乱花钱。

lines.en: But even when you do everything right, budget, plan and make reasonable choices,
lines.zh: 但就算你啥都做对了，预算、计划、选择都合理

lines.en: debt has a way of surprising you.
lines.zh: 债务还是会突然冒出来

questions.question: What's the main point of the speaker's message?
options: A. Debt can affect anyone, even those who manage money carefully.
B. Most debt comes from people making poor spending choices.
C. Only young people and retirees face serious debt problems.
D. Credit card debt is the only type worth worrying about.
questions.explanation_zh: 讲话者强调债务可能发生在任何人身上，即使他们理财很谨慎。

questions.question: How does the speaker seem to feel about debt?
options: A. It's mostly avoidable if you plan well.
B. It's a common and sometimes unavoidable challenge.
C. It's only a problem for irresponsible spenders.
D. It's not as serious as people think.
questions.explanation_zh: 讲话者认为债务很普遍，有时即使计划周全也无法避免。

clips.title: 谎言也能教人诚实？
clips.info_takeaway: 乔治·华盛顿樱桃树故事其实是虚构，用谎言教人诚实，揭示诚实与欺骗动机的复杂性。

source.episode: When It's Okay to Lie

lines.en: A young George Washington chops down his father's prized cherry tree.
lines.zh: 年轻的乔治·华盛顿砍倒了父亲心爱的樱桃树。

lines.en: When his father confronts him about the deed, young George doesn't hide.
lines.zh: 父亲质问他时，小乔治没有躲。

lines.en: He doesn't deflect.
lines.zh: 他没推卸责任。

lines.en: He doesn't minimize.
lines.zh: 也没轻描淡写。

lines.en: I cannot tell a lie, he says.
lines.zh: 他说：我不能撒谎。

lines.en: I did cut it.
lines.zh: 是我砍的。

lines.en: Instead of scolding him, George's father embraces him.
lines.zh: 父亲没有责骂他，反而抱住了他。

lines.en: He declares that his son's honesty is worth more than a thousand trees.
lines.zh: 他说儿子的诚实比一千棵树都珍贵。

lines.en: This tale, first popularized in the early 19th century,
lines.zh: 这个故事在19世纪初开始流行。

lines.en: is one of America's most cherished moral parables.
lines.zh: 它是美国最受珍视的道德寓言之一。

lines.en: This is Washington's birthday, and the truthful he of the cherry tree
lines.zh: 今天是华盛顿的生日，那位樱桃树的诚实先生

lines.en: would look down on me and frown.
lines.zh: 会对我皱眉不满。

lines.en: It celebrates honesty and the courage that often accompanies it.
lines.zh: 这是在庆祝诚实和常伴随的勇气。

lines.en: Yet the story's enduring power rests on a deep irony.
lines.zh: 不过这个故事的力量其实源于一种讽刺。

lines.en: The parable is almost certainly a fabrication,
lines.zh: 这个寓言几乎肯定是编出来的，

lines.en: a lie invented to teach the importance of telling the truth.
lines.zh: 一个为了教人诚实而编的谎言。

lines.en: The myth-makers behind the story believe that fiction could serve a higher truth,
lines.zh: 编故事的人相信虚构能传达更高的真理，

lines.en: that people might be inspired to be truthful
lines.zh: 让人们受到启发去说真话，

lines.en: by the story of a hero who could not tell a lie.
lines.zh: 因为有个英雄从不撒谎的故事。

lines.en: The story of George Washington and the cherry tree
lines.zh: 这是关于华盛顿和樱桃树的故事

lines.en: reminds us that the motivations behind honesty and deception
lines.zh: 提醒我们，诚实和欺骗背后的动机

lines.en: are rarely straightforward.
lines.zh: 很少是单纯的。

lines.en: Lies can sometimes uphold moral ideals,
lines.zh: 谎言有时也能维护道德理想，

lines.en: and truths can sometimes be wielded to wound.
lines.zh: 真话有时也会被用来伤人。

lines.en: Today on the show, and in a companion episode on Hidden Brain Plus,
lines.zh: 今天的节目，还有 Hidden Brain Plus 的配套一期，

lines.en: can engaging in deception ever be the right thing to do?
lines.zh: 欺骗有时会是正确的选择吗？

lines.en: The Psychology of Good Lies, this week on Hidden Brain.
lines.zh: 《好谎言的心理学》，本周在 Hidden Brain 播出。

questions.question: What's the main idea of the passage?
options: A. A famous story about George Washington shows how honesty can be taught through fiction.
B. George Washington was always truthful and never lied in real life.
C. The cherry tree story is about the importance of planting trees.
D. Washington’s father valued trees more than honesty.
questions.explanation_zh: 文章主要讲述乔治·华盛顿樱桃树的故事，并指出这个虚构故事用谎言来传递诚实的价值。

questions.question: What is the speaker's takeaway about honesty and deception?
options: A. Sometimes lies can serve moral purposes and truths can cause harm.
B. All lies are bad and should be avoided at all costs.
C. Truth is always more important than any other value.
D. Deception never inspires people to be truthful.
questions.explanation_zh: 作者认为诚实和欺骗的动机并不简单，有时谎言可以维护道德理想，而真话也可能伤人。

clips.title: 美国在菲律宾的水刑与焚村
clips.info_takeaway: 1899年菲律宾反抗美国统治，美国军队采取焚村、集中营和类似水刑的酷刑手段。

source.episode: How the US became America

lines.en: It should be the earnest wish and paramount aim of the military administration
lines.zh: 军方的首要目标和真心愿望应该是

lines.en: to win the confidence, respect, and affection of the inhabitants of the Philippines.
lines.zh: 赢得菲律宾居民的信任、尊重和好感。

lines.en: President William McKinley.
lines.zh: 总统威廉·麦金莱。

lines.en: In February 1899, Filipinos began a new fight for independence.
lines.zh: 1899年2月，菲律宾人开始新的独立斗争。

lines.en: This time against the United States.
lines.zh: 这次是对抗美国。

lines.en: You know, you can imagine the dashed hopes of people who'd been fighting for independence.
lines.zh: 你可以想象，那些一直为独立而战的人希望破灭的心情。

lines.en: Suddenly you have to realize they have to do it again.
lines.zh: 突然他们意识到还得再来一次。

lines.en: And they do do it again.
lines.zh: 而他们确实又干了。

lines.en: This is Daniel Imarvar.
lines.zh: 我是丹尼尔·伊马尔瓦。

lines.en: I wrote a book called How to Hide an Empire, a History of the Greater United States.
lines.zh: 我写了一本书，叫《如何隐藏一个帝国：大美国的历史》。

lines.en: He's a history professor at Northwestern University.
lines.zh: 他是西北大学的历史教授。

lines.en: And it becomes a war that just consumes so many lives.
lines.zh: 这场战争吞噬了无数生命。

lines.en: I hope that when they are conquered, they will be made to see for many years
lines.zh: 我希望他们被征服后，能被迫认清很多年

lines.en: the iron hand of military rule, the only kind for which they are suited.
lines.zh: 只有铁腕的军管才适合他们。

lines.en: The United States is burning villages.
lines.zh: 美国在烧村子。

lines.en: U.S.
lines.zh: 美国

lines.en: forces are concentrating people in camps or garrison towns
lines.zh: 军队把人集中到营地或驻军城镇

lines.en: where they're cut off from food supplies.
lines.zh: 在那里他们被切断了粮食供应。

lines.en: It's torturing people with a kind of water-based torture
lines.zh: 他们用一种跟水有关的酷刑折磨人

lines.en: that bears a discomforting resemblance to waterboarding today.
lines.zh: 跟现在的水刑很像，让人很不舒服。

lines.en: Back then it was called the water cure, where dirty water was put into insurgents' mouths
lines.zh: 那时候叫水刑，就是把脏水灌进叛乱分子的嘴里

lines.en: or suspected mouths until they just sort of couldn't take it anymore
lines.zh: 或者怀疑的人嘴里，直到他们实在受不了为止

questions.question: What's the main point of this passage?
options: A. The U.S. fought a brutal war against Filipino independence fighters after 1899.
B. The Philippines peacefully joined the United States in the early 1900s.
C. The U.S. military helped rebuild Filipino villages after the war.
D. Filipinos welcomed U.S. rule and saw it as beneficial.
questions.explanation_zh: 文章主要讲的是1899年后美国与菲律宾独立斗士之间的残酷战争。

questions.question: How does the speaker seem to feel about the U.S. actions in the Philippines?
options: A. Critical of the cruelty and harsh military rule.
B. Proud of the military's discipline.
C. Neutral and focused only on facts.
D. Supportive of the U.S. strategy.
questions.explanation_zh: 讲话者对美国在菲律宾的残酷行为持批评态度，强调了暴力和压制。

clips.title: 宽恕枪手引发争议
clips.info_takeaway: 他选择宽恕枪手，引发社区争议，强调沟通胜于对错之争。

source.episode: What it Takes to Heal

lines.en: I'm just processing.
lines.zh: 我只是还在消化。

lines.en: I think it's something that, while not every Pittsburgher was there, we all
lines.zh: 我觉得虽然不是所有匹兹堡人都在现场，但我们都

lines.en: were.
lines.zh: 在场。

lines.en: That means so much.
lines.zh: 这意义很大。

lines.en: It really does.
lines.zh: 真的很重要。

lines.en: Dan and Amber ended up having a lot to say to each
lines.zh: 丹和安珀最后聊了很多

lines.en: other.
lines.zh: 彼此的事。

lines.en: They talked for a full hour.
lines.zh: 他们聊了整整一个小时。

lines.en: Do you ever feel troubled by people of the same beliefs as
lines.zh: 你会被和你信仰一样的人困扰过吗

lines.en: you?
One of the things that I did as I was emerging from the ICU was to express my need to
lines.zh: 我从重症监护室出来时做的一件事，就是表达我需要

lines.en: forgive the shooter.
lines.zh: 原谅那个开枪的人。

lines.en: And this is something that some people completely disagreed with in my
lines.zh: 这事在我所在的社区里，有些人完全不同意。

lines.en: community.
lines.zh: 社区里的人。

lines.en: And it was interpreted that that need on my part to forgive this individual for what he
lines.zh: 有人觉得我想原谅他对我做的事，等于也原谅他杀了11个人。

lines.en: did to me also meant that I was forgiving him for killing 11 people.
lines.zh: 但事实不是这样。

lines.en: And that's not the case.
lines.zh: 我的态度不是要说服别人我是对的你是错的，因为

lines.en: My attitude about that is not to convince people that I'm right and you're wrong, because that
lines.zh: 那根本行不通。

lines.en: doesn't work.
lines.zh: 现在这种对立已经太多了。

lines.en: We have way too much of that these days.
lines.zh: 我们现在这种对立已经太多了。

lines.en: And that's unfortunately what led somebody
lines.zh: 不幸的是，这就是导致有人

lines.en: to come into my synagogue and do what he did.
lines.zh: 闯进我的犹太教堂做出那事的原因。

lines.en: I just really wish that he had come in in frustration
lines.zh: 我真希望他是带着挫败感进来

lines.en: and said, I want to talk to you rather than I want to shoot you.
lines.zh: 说想跟我聊聊，而不是想开枪。

questions.question: What's the main point the speaker is making?
options: A. They value forgiveness but see it as personal, not excusing wider harm.
B. They want everyone in Pittsburgh to agree on beliefs.
C. They believe disagreements should be avoided entirely.
D. They think the shooter acted out of religious conviction.
questions.explanation_zh: 讲话者强调自己对枪手的个人原谅是出于自身需要，并不意味着宽恕他造成的其他伤害。

questions.question: How does the speaker feel about handling disagreements?
options: A. It's better to have open conversations than to force your view.
B. Strong arguments are the best way to change minds.
C. Disagreements should be ignored to avoid conflict.
D. People with different beliefs should be kept apart.
questions.explanation_zh: 讲话者认为与其强行说服别人，不如通过开放的交流来处理分歧。
